user_story: As a project manager, I want to start a new project by entering the required project details, optionally providing a stop‑word file and selecting a weighting option (TF‑IDF or Okapi‑LTU), with the ability to cancel the process before the project is created, so that I can configure the project accurately and ensure it is created in the correct mode (Project or Trace) or not created at all if I decide to abort.
true_user_story: The START NEW PROJECT function shall allow the user to create a new project.

user_story: As a project manager, I want to start a new project by entering a project name and a low‑level document path, optionally selecting an IR method (Vector‑Space Retrieval, Keyword Extraction, or Keyword Extraction‑IDF) and a vocabulary base (Both Documents or Low Level Document Only), and if I choose Keyword Extraction or Keyword Extraction‑IDF I can set a weight threshold, so that I can load the RTM and configure the project for analysis.
true_user_story: The user shall be prompted to provide the new project name.

user_story: As a project manager, I want to start a new project by entering the project name, low‑level document path, high‑level document path, RTM file path, and optionally a thesaurus filename, so that the system creates the project in Trace mode with all required documents correctly linked and ready for use.
true_user_story: The user shall be prompted to provide the low level document path.

user_story: As a project initiator, I want to start a new project by entering the project name and specifying the paths to the low‑level document, high‑level document, and RTM file, and optionally selecting a weight threshold and vocabulary base, so that the system can configure the project environment and prepare the necessary documents for analysis.
true_user_story: The user shall be prompted to provide the high level document.

user_story: As a user, I want to be prompted to select an IR method from the list (Vector‑Space Retrieval, Keyword Extraction, Keyword Extraction‑IDF), and if I choose Keyword Extraction or Keyword Extraction‑IDF I want to be prompted to set a weight threshold, optionally choose a vocabulary base (Both Documents or Low Level Document Only, defaulting to Both Documents), and I want the ability to cancel the new‑project process at any time before the project is created, so that I can configure the project exactly to my needs and abort if I change my mind.
true_user_story: The user shall optionally be prompted to select an IR Method from the following list: Vector-Space Retrieval Keyword Extraction Keyword Extraction-IDF

user_story: As a data analyst, I want to start a new project where I can (1) optionally select a vocabulary base of either **Both Documents** or **Low Level Document Only** (defaulting to **Both Documents**), (2) optionally choose a weight option from **TF‑IDF** (default) or **Okapi LTU**, and (3) optionally pick an IR method from **Vector‑Space Retrieval**, **Keyword Extraction**, or **Keyword Extraction‑IDF**. The project should then be created automatically in **Trace** mode. This will allow me to configure the keyword‑extraction pipeline to match my data set and analysis goals while ensuring the project is ready for detailed tracking and debugging.
true_user_story: The user shall optionally be prompted to select a Vocabulary Base of either “Both Documents” or “Low Level Document Only”.  The default value is “Both Documents”.

user_story: As a user, I want to be prompted to select a weight option (TF‑IDF, Okapi, or LTU) when I choose an IR method of Keyword Extraction or Keyword Extraction‑IDF, so that I can apply the appropriate weighting scheme to the extracted keywords.
true_user_story: The user shall optionally be prompted to provide Weight Option from the following list: TF-IDF (Default) Okapi LTU

user_story: As a user, I want the system to require me to enter a weight threshold whenever I select an IR Method of "Keyword Extraction" or "Keyword Extraction-IDF", so that the project is configured with an appropriate threshold for keyword extraction and can be created successfully in Trace mode.
true_user_story: If the user has selected an IR Method of Keyword Extraction or Keyword Extraction-IDF, the user must also select a Weight Threshold.

user_story: As a user, I want to be optionally prompted to provide a Thesaurus filename and a Stop‑Word filename when I start a new project, so that I can customize the lexical resources used for text analysis and tailoring the project to my specific terminology and stop‑word requirements.
true_user_story: The user shall optionally be prompted to provide a Thesaurus Filename.

user_story: As a user, I want to be prompted to provide a new project name, an optional thesaurus filename, an optional stop‑word file, and an optional weight option, so that I can configure and create my new project with the desired settings and have it placed in Trace mode.
true_user_story: The user shall optionally be prompted to provide the Stop Word file.

user_story: As a user, I want the system to optionally prompt me to provide a Feedback Method when I start a new project, so that I can choose how feedback is collected (currently only the “Standard Rochio” option) and have the project configured accordingly.
true_user_story: The user shall optionally be prompted to provide a Feedback Method.  “Standard Rochio” is currently the only option.

user_story: As a user, I want to be able to cancel the new‑project creation process at any point before the project is actually created, so that I can return to the previous state and avoid creating an unintended project.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to cancel the process and return to the previous state at any time before the project is created.

user_story: As a user, I want to start a new project by entering the required input fields, selecting the desired project mode (Trace, Assess, or any other specified mode), and optionally providing a Stop‑Word file, a Thesaurus file, and a Feedback Method, so that the project is created in the correct mode and is immediately ready for further analysis or loading.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to create the project using the above input.  The project shall be placed in “Trace” mode.

user_story: As a user, I want to load an existing project from the saved projects directory, with the ability to cancel the action, select a feedback method (Standard Rochio), and load the project in its last‑saved state with all associated mode options, so that I can seamlessly continue my work without losing progress or configuration.
true_user_story: Since the majority of projects are not completed in a single setting, then it is critical to provide the user the option to load an existing project to continue working on the project.  Load Project feature will provide the user the capacity to open an existing project in the last saved state with all the associated mode options. [1]

user_story: As a project manager, I want to load an existing project by selecting its name from the list of saved projects, clicking the **Load RTM** prompt, entering a new project name and the low‑level document path, and selecting the only available feedback method (**Standard Rochio**), so that I can resume work on the correct project configuration quickly and have the option to cancel the load action and return to the previous screen if needed.
true_user_story: The user shall be prompted to select a project name from a list of saved projects.  Project names are obtained by scanning the saved projects directory.

user_story: As a user, I want to be optionally prompted to select a Feedback Method when loading a project, so that I can choose the feedback approach (currently only "Standard Rochio") or skip the prompt and use the default without extra steps.
true_user_story: The user shall be prompted to select a feedback method.  The only option is “Standard Rochio”.

user_story: As a user, I want to be able to cancel the Load Project action and return to the previous activity, and to load a project once I have selected a project name and feedback method, so that I can control the project‑loading process and avoid unintended project creation.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to cancel the LOAD PROJECT action and return to the previous activity.

user_story: As a user, I want to load an existing project by selecting its name from a list and choosing the feedback method, with the ability to cancel and return to the previous screen, so that I can resume work on the project in Trace mode.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to load the project once the project name and feedback method is selected.

user_story: As a project manager, I want to load an existing Requirements Traceability Matrix (RTM) into the system by selecting the IR method, specifying a weight threshold if the method is Keyword Extraction or Keyword Extraction‑IDF, optionally providing a thesaurus file, and choosing the project mode, so that I can initialize a new project with the loaded RTM and continue the assessment process.
true_user_story: Similar in functionality to the START NEW PROJECT function, the LOAD RTM function collects project parameters from the user and initializes the project.  A project created via the LOAD RTM function may be in either “trace” or “assess” mode.  Assess mode is usually used when the user is provided a RTM and needs to evaluate its accuracy, completeness, and satisfaction level.

user_story: As a user, I want to load an RTM by entering a project name, specifying the low‑level document path, optionally providing a stop‑word file, and selecting either trace or assess mode, so that the system can initialize the project with the correct parameters and prepare it for evaluation or analysis.
true_user_story: The user shall be prompted to provide the new project name.

user_story: As a user, I want to load an RTM by providing the new project name, low‑level document path, high‑level document path, and RTM file path, optionally selecting a feedback method and a weight option, and be able to cancel the process before the project is created, so that I can configure the project accurately and retain full control over the setup.
true_user_story: The user shall be prompted to provide the low level document path.

user_story: As a project analyst, I want to load an RTM file into a new project by being prompted to provide the new project name, low‑level document path, high‑level document path, and RTM file path—optionally selecting an IR method and the mode (trace or assess)—so that I can initialize the project and evaluate the RTM’s accuracy, completeness, and satisfaction level.
true_user_story: The user shall be prompted to provide the high level document path.

user_story: As a user, I want to be prompted to provide the high‑level document path, the RTM file path, and optionally select an IR method, weight option, thesaurus filename, and new project name, so that I can load the RTM correctly and configure the analysis settings for my project.
true_user_story: The user shall be prompted to provide the RTM file path.

user_story: As a user, I want to be optionally prompted to select an IR Method (Vector‑Space Retrieval, Keyword Extraction, or Keyword Extraction‑IDF) when loading an RTM, so that I can choose the most appropriate information‑retrieval approach for my project.
true_user_story: The user shall optionally be prompted to select an IR Method from the following list: Vector-Space Retrieval Keyword Extraction Keyword Extraction-IDF

user_story: As a user, I want to be prompted to select the vocabulary base (either **Both Documents** or **Low Level Document Only**) when loading an RTM, with **Both Documents** set as the default, so that I can control the scope of keyword extraction and tailor the analysis to the specific documents I am working with.
true_user_story: The user shall optionally be prompted to select a Vocabulary Base of either “Both Documents” or “Low Level Document Only”.  The default value is “Both Documents”.

user_story: As a user, I want to be prompted to select a weight option (TF‑IDF, Okapi, or LTU) when loading an RTM file, so that I can control the weighting scheme used for keyword extraction and ensure that a weight is chosen whenever the IR method is Keyword Extraction or Keyword Extraction‑IDF. The default weight should be TF‑IDF, and the prompt should appear only after the RTM file path, vocabulary base, and low‑level document path have been provided.
true_user_story: The user shall optionally be prompted to provide “Weight Option” from the following list: TF-IDF (Default) Okapi LTU

user_story: As a Data Analyst, I want the system to prompt me to select a Weight Threshold whenever I choose Keyword Extraction or Keyword Extraction‑IDF as the IR Method, so that I can ensure the keyword extraction process is appropriately tuned for my analysis.
true_user_story: If the user has selected an IR Method of “Keyword Extraction” or “Keyword Extraction-IDF”, the user must also select a “Weight Threshold”.

user_story: As a user, I want to be prompted to optionally provide a Thesaurus filename and a Stop‑Word filename when I load an RTM, so that I can customize the vocabulary and filtering for the project and tailor the analysis to my domain.
true_user_story: The user shall optionally be prompted to provide a “Thesaurus” filename.

user_story: As a data analyst, I want to be prompted to optionally provide a thesaurus file and a stop‑word file when loading the RTM, so that I can customize the vocabulary and filtering for my domain and improve the accuracy of the analysis.
true_user_story: The user shall optionally be prompted to provide the “Stop Word” filename.

user_story: As a user, I want to be optionally prompted to provide a Feedback Method, so that I can specify the method the system should use for processing feedback (currently only the Standard Rochio option is available). This optional prompt will allow me to tailor the feedback workflow to my needs while keeping the interface simple when I choose to accept the default. The system should present the prompt only when the user has not already selected a method, and it should accept the single available option, "Standard Rochio", without requiring any additional configuration.
true_user_story: The user shall optionally be prompted to provide a “Feedback Method”.  “Standard Rochio” is currently the only option.

user_story: As a user, I want to load an RTM and be prompted to select the Project Mode (Trace or Assess), the IR Method (Vector‑Space Retrieval, Keyword Extraction, Keyword Extraction‑IDF), and the Weight Option (TF‑IDF, Okapi LTU), so that I can configure the project for the appropriate analysis workflow and evaluate the RTM’s accuracy, completeness, and satisfaction level when in Assess mode.
true_user_story: The user shall optionally be prompted to provide the “Project Mode” of either “Trace” or “Assess”.  “Trace” is the default mode.

user_story: As a user, I want to be able to cancel the Load RTM process at any time before the project is created, so that I can return to the previous state without creating a new project.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to cancel the process and return to the previous state at any time before the project is created.

user_story: As a project manager, I want to load an existing RTM project by selecting its name and mode, so that I can resume work with the correct description, priority, and mode settings.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to create the project using the above input.  The project shall be placed in specified “Project Mode”.

user_story: As a project user, I want to activate the SAVE function to save the current data and status of the project, so that I can preserve my work and resume later without loss.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to activate the SAVE function to save the current data and status of the project.

user_story: As a user, I want to be able to save the current project via the File menu and close it with a prompt to confirm saving, so that all my work is preserved and I can exit the application safely.
true_user_story: When activated RETRO will save all current data and project.

user_story: As a user of the RETRO application, I want to be able to close the current project from the File menu without exiting the application, so that I can start a new project or exit the program without losing my work or having to navigate away from the current workspace.
true_user_story: The CLOSE CURRENT PROJECT Function will allow the user to close a given project without exiting the RETRO application.

user_story: As a user of the RETRO application, I want to close the current project without exiting the program, so that I can quickly switch to another project or continue working on other tasks while keeping the application running.
true_user_story: The user shall be able close the current project.

user_story: As a user, I want to exit the RETRO application, so that I can close the application when I am finished working.
true_user_story: It is necessary to provide a mechanism to exit the RETRO application when the user is finished working with the application.

user_story: As a user of the RETRO application, I want a clear exit mechanism that lets me close the application or cancel the current process and return to the previous state, so that I can end my session or abort a project creation without losing my work.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to exit the application.

user_story: As a user, I want to open the Action menu and select Show XML, so that I can view the current project’s XML file format—including the list of high‑ and low‑connection elements—in a new screen for inspection, debugging, and verification.
true_user_story: The user may want to view the current project’s XML file format.  The SHOW XML function will provide the user a display of the current XML file.

user_story: As a project analyst, I want to view the XML version of the RTM and generate a report with the RTM With Text option, so that I can verify the RTM structure and produce a readable report for stakeholders. The system will prompt me to provide the high‑level document path and the RTM file path, then display the XML view and generate the report in a new screen for easy review.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to view the XML version of the RTM.

user_story: As a project analyst, I want to generate and save the **Project Trace Report** from the list of available reports, and modify its design after the trace process completes, so that I can review trace results and share the customized report with stakeholders.
true_user_story: After a project has completed either a trace or an assess process, then the user will need the capability to review the final results of the process in an easy to read format other than the XML file format that is automatically generated throughout the process.  The GENERATE REPORT function will provide the user a list of reports that the user may view.  The user will have the option to save the generated report or modify the design of the report. [1]

user_story: As a project manager, I want to generate and save RTM reports with the text option and parent‑with‑no‑children reports from the Action menu, so that I can review traceability and requirement coverage in an easy‑to‑read format and keep a record of the final results without relying on XML files.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to generate and save the “RTM report”

user_story: As a project analyst, I want to generate and save the **Children with no parents** report from the Action menu, with the option to modify its design, so that I can quickly identify orphan child records and present the results in an easy‑to‑read format for stakeholders.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to generate and save the “Parent with no children report”

user_story: As a system user, I want to generate and save the **Parents with no children with text report** from the Action menu, so that I can identify parents who have no associated children and take appropriate corrective action. The report should open in a new screen and be available for download or printing. This feature will help maintain data integrity and support downstream processes that rely on accurate parent‑child relationships.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to generate and save the “Parents with no children with text report”

user_story: As a project manager, I want to generate and save the various predefined reports (RTM report, Parent with no children report, Parents with no children report, Parents with no children with text report, Children with no parents report, Children with no parents with text report, and Not satisfied requirements report) so that I can review the final results of the trace or assess process in an easy‑to‑read format, share them with stakeholders, and modify the report design if needed.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to generate and save the “Children with no parents report”

user_story: As a project analyst, I want to generate and save the following reports from the Action menu—Parents with no children with text report, Children with no parents report, Children with no parents with text report, and Not satisfied requirements report—so that I can review the final results of trace or assess processes in an easy‑to‑read format, modify the report design if needed, and keep a saved copy for future reference.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to generate and save the “Children with no parents with text report”

user_story: As a project manager, I want to generate and save a variety of reports—such as the Not Satisfied Requirements with Text Report, Children with No Parents Text Report, Not Satisfied Requirements Report, RTM Report, and Parent with No Children Report—using a single Generate Report function that presents a list of available reports, allows me to filter low‑level requirements by description and priority, and provides a checkbox for Not Satisfied Requirements that opens a dedicated screen for that report. I also want the ability to modify the design of each report before saving it, so that I can review the final results of trace or assess processes in an easy‑to‑read format and keep a record of the output for future reference.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to generate and save the “Not satisfied requirements report”

user_story: As a project analyst, I want to generate and save a range of text‑based reports—specifically the "Not satisfied requirements with text" report, the "Children with no parents" report, and the "Not satisfied requirements" report—while also being able to filter low‑level requirements and adjust the report design, so that I can review and share clear, readable summaries of the project’s traceability and assessment results without having to parse raw XML files.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to generate and save the “Not satisfied requirements with text report”

user_story: As a Requirements Analyst, I want to filter the list of low‑level requirements and candidate requirement links by weight and scope (local or global), so that I can limit the display to only the most relevant requirements for a selected high‑level element while ensuring that no required low‑level requirement is omitted.
true_user_story: “The user shall be able to limit the display of low-level requirements or candidate requirement links.” [1]

user_story: As a requirements analyst, I want to designate whether the low‑level requirement filter applies to the selected high‑level requirement (LOCAL) or to all high‑level requirements (GLOBAL) from the Trace or Assess tab, so that I can either narrow my analysis to a specific requirement hierarchy or apply a consistent filter across the entire model.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to designate whether the filter process applies to the selected high level document element (LOCAL) or to all high level document elements (GLOBAL).

user_story: As a requirements analyst, I want to filter the list of low‑level requirements by setting a weight cutoff between 0 and 1.0, limit the display to the top N elements, sort the results by weight, recency or alphabetically, and activate a “KILL HIDDEN LINKS” function to remove all elements not currently displayed, so that I can focus on the most relevant requirements and keep the view uncluttered.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to filter the list of low-level requirements by setting a cutoff point for the weight between 0 and 1.0.

user_story: As a Requirements Analyst, I want to filter low‑level requirements by setting a weight threshold (0–1.0) with a slider, specifying a top‑N limit via a radio option, choosing whether the filter applies to the selected high‑level element (LOCAL) or to all high‑level elements (GLOBAL), and optionally activating a KILL HIDDEN Links function to remove all elements not currently displayed, so that I can focus on the most critical requirements and keep the view uncluttered.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to designate that the top N elements to be displayed.

user_story: As a requirements analyst, I want to activate the **Kill Hidden Links** function to remove all low‑level requirement elements that are not currently displayed, with the option to apply the filter to either the selected high‑level element (LOCAL) or to all high‑level elements (GLOBAL). This will allow me to focus on the relevant requirements and candidate links without visual clutter, improving my ability to review and manage the requirement hierarchy efficiently.
true_user_story: The user shall be able activate a KILL HIDDEN Links function to remove all elements not currently displayed.

user_story: As a requirements analyst, I want to sort low‑level documents by **Requirement ID**, **Document Order**, and **Weights** in the display, so that I can quickly locate, review, and prioritize the low‑level requirements.
true_user_story: The user will need the ability to view the text of low-level requirements.  The DISPLAY function will provide three different sort options to view the text of low-level requirements.  The user can sort the low-level requirements based upon the weight of linking relationships, original document order, or by requirement id. [1]

user_story: As a system user, I want to be able to sort low‑level documents by Requirement ID, Weights, and Document Order using a simple radio‑button interface, so that I can quickly locate and analyze specific low‑level elements in the order that best suits my workflow.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to sort the low level documents by “Requirement ID”

user_story: As a requirements analyst, I want to sort low‑level documents by Requirement ID, Weight, and Document Order, so that I can quickly locate and review specific low‑level requirements in the order that best supports my analysis.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to sort the low level documents by “Weights”

user_story: As a user, I want to sort the low‑level documents by **Document Order**, so that I can view them in the original sequence of the source document for easier navigation and reference.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to sort the low level documents by “Document Order”

user_story: As a system analyst, I want to select a low‑level requirement and, while in **Assess** mode, view its link status (Link / No Link / Default) and trigger an automatic accuracy & completeness check that incorporates user feedback, so that I can determine whether the tracing was successful and use that insight to continuously improve the RETRO system.
true_user_story: Since RETRO’s tracing and assessing modes are based upon algorithms that will allow processes to be improved as the system receives FEEDBACK from the user.

user_story: As a Requirements Analyst, I want to be able to right‑click a low‑level requirement, change its link status (Link / No Link / Default) via a context menu, and quickly delete all requirements that are marked **No Link**, so that I can provide accurate, actionable feedback to the RETRO system and help it refine its tracing and assessment algorithms for improved project management.
true_user_story: The user shall select a low level requirement and identify the link status from the following list: Link No Link Default

user_story: As a user of the RETRO System, I want to be able to load and keep multiple projects open at the same time—including loading an existing project to continue working—so that I can switch between ongoing projects without losing context and resume work seamlessly.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to load multiple projects at the same time.

user_story: As a user, I want to open and manage multiple projects simultaneously—reopening each in its last‑saved state with all mode options and closing individual projects without exiting the RETRO application—so that I can work on several projects concurrently, maintain continuity, and avoid restarting the system.
true_user_story: The RETRO System shall allow multiple projects to be open at the same time

user_story: As a user, I want to import existing project files (including converting plain‑text input files into the RETRO file format) and have them automatically created in Trace mode, and also be able to load projects after they have completed a trace or assess process, so that I can quickly start new tracing sessions and review or reprocess completed projects without manual setup.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to import projects.

user_story: As a user, I want to import an existing project by selecting the high‑level requirements file, the low‑level requirements file, and the RTM file, so that the RETRO system can prompt me for the file paths, convert the input text files into RETRO format, and load the project into the system for further analysis.
true_user_story: The RETRO System shall provide a mechanism to convert existing input text files into RETRO file format.  (Not implemented.)

user_story: As a user of the RETRO System, I want a stable, user‑friendly GUI that displays all labels and documentation with correct spelling and provides an easy way to view each individual requirement, so that I can interact with the system efficiently and confidently without encountering crashes or confusion.
true_user_story: The user shall use a GUI INTERFACE to work with RETRO.

user_story: As a RETRO user, I want a stable, well‑labeled GUI interface that does not crash, so that I can interact with the system efficiently and confidently without encountering errors or mis‑spelled labels.
true_user_story: The RETRO System shall provide a GUI mechanism for the user to interact with the RETRO System.

user_story: As a user, I want to use the keyword search feature in Browse mode to search both high‑level and low‑level requirements, so that I can quickly locate the specific requirement I need.
true_user_story: The user is provided the capability via BROWSE mode to manually link low-level requirements to high-level requirement. In order to help the user to quickly locate the desired low-level requirement or high-level requirement, a KEYWORD SEARCH function is offered to the user. [1]

user_story: As a Requirements Engineer, I want to enter a keyword into a search text box to search within both low‑level and high‑level requirements, so that I can quickly locate and retrieve the relevant requirements for review, linking, and traceability.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to enter a keyword into a search text box to search within low-level requirements and the system shall return matches.

user_story: As a requirements analyst, I want to enter a keyword into a search text box that searches both high‑level and low‑level requirements and returns matching results, so that I can quickly locate the desired requirements and facilitate manual linking or further analysis.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to enter a keyword into a search text box to search within high-level requirements and the system shall return matches.

user_story: As a requirement analyst, I want to manually link low‑level requirements to high‑level requirements using RETRO’s browse mode, be able to mark candidate links as "No Link" and delete them, and also create or remove low‑level requirements in relation to high‑level ones, so that I can maintain accurate, up‑to‑date traceability and ensure that only valid links remain in the system.
true_user_story: There will be times when the user will not agree with RETRO’s list of candidate links.  Therefore, it is important to provide a mechanism for the user to manually change, create, or delete links between low and high level requirements.  BROWSE mode is the mechanism within RETRO that enables the user to perform MANUAL LINKING/TRACING. [1]

user_story: As a requirement analyst, I want to be able to filter the display of low‑level requirements and their candidate links in BROWSE mode, so that I can focus on the most relevant items and manually create, modify, or delete links between low‑level and high‑level requirements.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to create and remove low-level requirements with high-level requirements.

user_story: As a requirements engineer, I want to generate a trace on all requirements or on a specific requirement, so that I can automatically link high‑level and low‑level requirements and see the candidate links between them.
true_user_story: One of the purposes of RETRO is to aid the user in linking low-level requirements with high-level requirements.  TRACING functionality is the component of RETRO that automatically generates a list of candidate links of high to low-level requirements.

user_story: As a requirements analyst, I want to generate a trace on all requirements or on a selected requirement(s), so that I can automatically generate and review candidate links between high‑level and low‑level requirements.
true_user_story: RETRO shall provide a mechanism for the user to generate a trace on all requirements.

user_story: As a requirements analyst, I want RETRO to provide a mechanism for me to generate a trace on a selected requirement or set of requirements, so that I can automatically see a list of candidate links between those low‑level requirements and their corresponding high‑level requirements and quickly validate the traceability.
true_user_story: RETRO shall provide a mechanism for the user to generate a trace on a given requirement(s).

user_story: As a Trace Analyst, I want to view the tracing results sorted from high‑to‑low level requirements, filter them by any criteria, provide feedback on the candidate links, and then complete the trace so that the project can be marked as finished and no further changes are allowed.
true_user_story: After the TRACING function is performed, then the results of the TRACING process will need to be made available to the user to view.  The TRACING RESULTS functionality will display a list of all candidate links of high to low-level requirements based on any given filtering criteria.  The user will be allowed to provide feedback on the TRACING RESULTS in order to improve future TRACING.

user_story: As a requirements engineer, I want to view the tracing results in RETRO, filter the list of candidate links by any criteria, and provide feedback on each link’s satisfaction level, so that I can quickly identify unsatisfactory links, prioritize review, and improve the accuracy of future tracing.
true_user_story: RETRO shall display the list of candidate links based on the tracing and permit feedback, filtering, and display functionality.

user_story: As a RETRO trace engineer, I want to be able to save the current status of a trace, so that I can preserve my work and resume the tracing process later without losing progress.
true_user_story: The SAVE TRACE function shall allow the user to save the trace result.

user_story: As a Trace Analyst, I want to generate a trace on all or selected requirements and then save the current status of that trace (including the chosen mode – Trace or Assess) so that I can preserve the trace results for future review, reporting, and audit purposes.
true_user_story: RETRO shall provide a mechanism to save the current status of trace.

user_story: As a Trace Manager, I want to complete the trace process so that the project is marked as completed, no further changes are permitted, and the current trace status is saved for audit and future reference.
true_user_story: The COMPLETING TRACE function shall allow user to finish the tracing process.

user_story: As a requirements engineer, I want the COMPLETING TRACE function to automatically save all current data and close the current project when I finish tracing, so that I can be confident that all low‑level requirements are correctly linked to the high‑level release and the project state is safely archived.
true_user_story: RETRO shall mark the project completed and not permit further changes.

user_story: As a Requirements Engineer, I want to run an accuracy & completeness assessment that performs a full trace on the requirements, compares the IR‑method results to the given RTM, and allows me to provide feedback on the tracing results, so that I can verify that tracing was successful, identify any missing or incorrect links, and continuously improve the tracing process.
true_user_story: The user needs a mechanism that will help to determine whether the TRACING was successfully performed based on accuracy and completeness.  An accuracy test tells the user that the candidate links provided are correctly related to the high-level requirement.  Completeness test tells the user that all possible links that are listed in the candidate list are included and no low-level requirements are missing. [1]

user_story: As a Requirements Analyst, I want to run an Accuracy & Completeness check from the Assess tab, so that I can verify that all trace links are correctly related to their high‑level requirements and that no low‑level requirements are missing, allowing me to identify recommended links and improve the RTM.
true_user_story: RETRO shall provide a mechanism for the user to be able to run an ACCURACY & COMPLETENESS check by performing a full trace on the requirements and comparing the IR method results to the given RTM. [1]

user_story: As a user, I want to view a list of recommendations generated by RETRO’s tracing process while in assess mode and provide feedback on each recommendation, so that I can validate the alignment of requirements with the RTM and improve overall traceability.
true_user_story: “The [user] will need to have a list of recommendations based on RETRO’s TRACING process.  SHOW RECOMMENDATION will display to the [user] a list of recommendation while in ASSESS mode.  The [user] will have the capability to provide FEEDBACK to the RETRO system on whether or not the [user] agrees with RETRO’s assessment of the linking between the low-level requirements to the high-level requirements.” [1]

user_story: As a user, I want to be able to display or hide the list of recommendations generated by RETRO’s tracing process while in ASSESS mode, and to provide feedback on each recommendation, so that I can evaluate the satisfaction analysis results against the given RTM and improve the linkage between low‑level and high‑level requirements.
true_user_story: RETRO shall display/hide recommendations at the user’s request.

user_story: As a stakeholder using RETRO, I want the tool to run a satisfaction analysis that calculates the percentage of satisfaction for each high‑level requirement based on its linked low‑level requirements and displays the result as “Satisfied”, “Partially Satisfied”, or “Not Satisfied”, so that I can quickly assess whether the implementation meets the high‑level goals and identify gaps that need attention.
true_user_story: Satisfaction function shows how satisfied the high-level requirement is with the given set of low-level requirements. [4] The Satisfaction Analysis function is checking the level of percentage for a satisfaction of the linked result with the high and low level input files. Once final output is agreed, the list of high level requirement is updated with options like “Satisfied”, “Not Satisfied” and “Partially Satisfied”. The status of high level requirement is color coded. Unsatisfied result can be repeated until all the high levels has been satisfied stage.

user_story: As a user of the RETRO tool, I want the system to display the percentage level of satisfaction for each link when I click the **Run Satisfaction Analysis** button, so that I can quickly identify which high‑level requirements are fully satisfied, partially satisfied, or not satisfied, and focus my effort on the links that need improvement.
true_user_story: RETRO shall display to the user the percentage level of link. [1]

user_story: As a requirements analyst, I want a **Remove All No Links** function available in ASSESS mode, so that I can quickly delete every low‑level requirement that is marked with the status *“No Link”* (and any incorrectly recommended links), keeping the requirement hierarchy clean, accurate, and focused on the high‑level goals.
true_user_story: While in ASSESS mode, the user will need a mechanism to allow the removal of all low-level requirements that are not linked to a high-level requirement.  REMOVE ALL NO LINKS function will provide an easy way for the user to quickly remove low-level requirements with status of “No Link”.

user_story: As a requirement analyst, I want to remove all low‑level requirements that are marked "No Link" from the system while in ASSESS mode, so that I can quickly clean up unlinked items and keep the requirement set focused on linked, high‑level requirements.
true_user_story: RETRO shall remove all links marked as “No Link”

user_story: As a user in ASSESS mode, I want to be able to select and delete any recommended link that I consider incorrect or irrelevant, so that my assessment view remains accurate, focused, and free of misleading connections.
true_user_story: While in ASSESS mode, the user will need a mechanism that will allow the deletion of recommended links that the user feels are not correctly linked.

user_story: As a content reviewer in ASSESS mode, I want to delete recommended links that are marked “No Link” or that I determine are incorrectly linked, so that the content remains accurate, relevant, and free of misleading or unnecessary references.
true_user_story: RETRO shall permit the user to select a recommended link and delete it.

user_story: As a user, I want each candidate link to be color‑coded according to its satisfaction status, so that I can quickly identify and prioritize links based on their satisfaction level.
true_user_story: “In order to help the user to quickly identify the level of satisfaction of each candidate link, RETRO will need to provide a visual aid.  COLOR-CODED SATISFACTION STATUS function will color code each candidate link based on its satisfaction level.   The various colors will be a visual aid to the user to help quickly identify each links satisfaction level.” [1]

user_story: As a user of RETRO, I want the system to color‑code each candidate link based on its satisfaction level, so that I can quickly identify and differentiate links by their satisfaction status.
true_user_story: RETRO shall COLOR-CODE THE SATISFACTION STATUS of each given candidate link.
